metadata.title:
ALL DEPENDS
ON GETTING


FRANCHISES

NORTH COAST AND THE |
MILWAUKEE LINES


MEAN BUSINESS |

ABOUT UNION DEPOT

pages.raw_text:
From officials Of the North Coast
railroad it is learned that an agree
ment has been reached with the
Milwaukee for the building Of a
union passenger terminal In Spo-
kane, whether any moro roads 90
into the proposition or not.


In pursuance of this agreement
the North Coast has asked the city
to sell the city hall property at the
corner of Front avenue and How
ard street at the city's price, $780,
600. The condition attached to the
purchase is that the city council
grant the North Coast a franchise
to reach It's terminal site. A new
franchise will be filed tonight.


It Is announced today that II the
franchise Is granted It will mean
the immediate investment of mil
lions for terminals and trackage In
Spokane and that work on bring
ing the two roads into the city will
begin at once.


Members Of the city council to-
day said that they want something
more than mere North Coast offer
as to union terminal If the city
property is to be sold. When the
price was set at $352,000 it was
upon the understanding that II the
railroads accepted II would be for
the purpose Of building a union
depot. Thus far the North Coast
seems to have been the leading
force In the union depot matter,
although the Milwaukee has rem
dered every service possible. Now
the councllmen want to be assured
that all this talk Of union depot
Is not merely North Coast play
to get tho city hall In order to com.
pIete the steps necessary as to Its
entrance into the city.


The union depot committee will
meet this afternoon to consider the
plans of the railroads, and the city
council will meet as a committee of
the whole tonight to consider the
franchise Of the North Coast and
any offer In writing that 1t may
make looking to the purchase Of the
&I(y hall.


Eyes, we have come to an agree.
ment with the Milwaukee for the
building Of union depot" said l..


(Continued on Paue Two.)
